fields.description:
## Context
A work ticket carries a single attestation slot (`state["signature"]`), written
last-writer-wins by `process_signature` (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`). The
plan-review attestation (claim time) and the completion verdict (close time) share that
slot, so closing destroys the plan-review attestation (bug grumpy-site-beard /
e6a0-ba9b-ae92-43d7). The two are independent proofs and must coexist; we also expect
further attestation kinds and per-ticket CI-gate consumption later.

## What
Replace the slot with `state["attestations"]`, a map keyed by attestation kind (per-key
LWW, mirroring `process_workflow_run`): additive across kinds, latest within a kind. Kind
is derived from the already-signed `manifest[0]` (`plan-review` / `completion-verifier`),
so no `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump and existing signatures still verify. Reuse the `SIGNATURE`
event (add an unsigned `data["kind"]` for robust routing). Verification is per-kind.
Attestations are immutable signed records; validity for a gate is computed on read via a
single `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` dispatcher.

## Migration / back-out (expand -> contract)
- New code writes ONLY `state["attestations"]` in compiled state and writes ONLY plain
  `SIGNATURE` events (with `data["kind"]`); it never writes `state["signature"]` into events.
- Read fold-in: a ticket/snapshot carrying only the legacy `state["signature"]` is folded
  into `attestations` on read (kind from its manifest).
- SNAPSHOT mirror (expand phase): when NEW code writes a SNAPSHOT it ALSO synthesizes a
  legacy `state["signature"]` (= the most-recent attestation) so a rolled-back or
  not-yet-upgraded clone reading that snapshot still sees one attestation. A named cutover
  release drops the mirror (contract phase). This is the rollback path.

## Validity model (computed on read; records never mutated)
- `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` centralizes per-kind rules; the
  "material change" field set is a named constant.
- completion-verifier valid iff: certified AND status==closed AND not reopened since
  `signed_at` AND material fingerprint unchanged since `signed_at`.
- plan-review valid iff: its existing freshness (material fingerprint + code drift) AND not
  reopened since `signed_at`.
- MATERIAL CHANGE = a post-`signed_at` mutation to any field in the plan-review material set
  (description, acceptance-criteria, file_impact, child set). Tag/assignee/comment/link
  changes are NOT material. The completion signer records this fingerprint in its manifest
  so the check is symmetric with plan-review.
- `retire_attested_pin` (the blank-`SIGNATURE`-on-reopen mutation) is REMOVED; reopen
  invalidation is handled entirely by validity-on-read for both kinds.

## Success Criteria
- A ticket simultaneously holds plan-review AND completion attestations; signing one never
  clobbers the other; re-running a kind replaces only that kind.
- Compaction/SNAPSHOT round-trips all kinds; legacy single-signature tickets and old-clone
  snapshots still read and verify; a new snapshot is readable by old code via the mirror.
- A reopened ticket stops reading as completion-valid (and as plan-review-valid) with no
  record mutation; re-closing/re-reviewing re-signs the relevant kind.

## Validation
- Manual dogfood smoke-test: create a ticket, sign a plan-review PASS, sign a
  completion-verifier PASS, run `rebar show` and confirm BOTH kinds appear with neither
  overwriting the other; reopen and confirm completion reads not-valid; verify each kind via
  `rebar verify-signature --kind K`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `state["attestations"]` (kind-keyed, per-key LWW) replaces the single slot; additive
      across kinds, replace within a kind.
- [ ] `process_signature` routes by `data["kind"]`, falling back to parsing `manifest[0]`.
- [ ] The signed canonical payload is unchanged; every pre-existing signature still certifies.
- [ ] New code never writes `state["signature"]` into events; read fold-in covers legacy
      tickets/old-clone snapshots; new SNAPSHOTs synthesize the legacy `signature` mirror;
      a named cutover release drops the mirror.
- [ ] `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` centralizes per-kind validity;
      MATERIAL-CHANGE field set is a named constant.
- [ ] completion validity = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND
      material-unchanged-since `signed_at`; the completion signer records the material
      fingerprint in its manifest.
- [ ] `retire_attested_pin` is removed; reopen invalidation is validity-on-read for both kinds.
- [ ] `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=...)` certifies one kind; no kind returns
      `{kind: verdict}`; claim gate reads `plan-review`, close + child-closure read
      `completion-verifier`.
- [ ] `show`/MCP render the `attestations` map (omitted when empty).
- [ ] Tests: coexistence; within-kind replace; compaction round-trip; legacy + new-snapshot
      cross-read; reopen invalidates completion (and plan-review) validity without mutation;
      material-change invalidates completion; concurrent different-kind signs both survive.
